questionHtml:
What is a function of DNA polymerase I?

A. Adds nucleotides in a 5’ to 3’ direction to elongate the chain

B. Uncoils the DNA double helix and splits it into two template strands

C. Removes RNA primer and replaces it with DNA

D. Produces sugar-phosphate bonds to link Okazaki fragments

meta.title: What is a function of DNA polymerase I? A. Adds nucleotides in a 5’ to 3’ direction to elongate the chain B. Uncoils the DNA double helix and splits it into two template strands C.
breadcrumbLabels: D1.1.3. Role of helicase and DNA polymerase in DNA replication
D1.1.8. Functions of DNA primase, DNA polymerase I, DNA polymerase III and DNA ligase in replication
